texts.label: title
texts.orig: Sample Bullet and List Document
texts.text: Sample Bullet and List Document

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This document contains various list types for testing list detection, indentation handling, and list-to-HTML conversion in document processing workflows.
texts.text: This document contains various list types for testing list detection, indentation handling, and list-to-HTML conversion in document processing workflows.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Basic Bulleted List
texts.text: 1. Basic Bulleted List

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following is a standard bulleted list:
texts.text: The following is a standard bulleted list:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: PDF is the most widely used document format worldwide.
texts.text: PDF is the most widely used document format worldwide.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: DOCX is the default format for Microsoft Word 2007 and later.
texts.text: DOCX is the default format for Microsoft Word 2007 and later.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: RTF provides cross-platform text formatting compatibility.
texts.text: RTF provides cross-platform text formatting compatibility.

texts.orig: TXT is the simplest document format with no formatting.
texts.text: TXT is the simplest document format with no formatting.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ODT is the open-source OpenDocument Text format.
texts.text: ODT is the open-source OpenDocument Text format.

texts.text: EPUB is designed for reflowable electronic publications.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Definition-Style List
texts.text: 5. Definition-Style List

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A list of terms and their definitions, commonly found in glossaries and technical documentation:
texts.text: A list of terms and their definitions, commonly found in glossaries and technical documentation:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: OOXML (Office Open XML)
texts.text: OOXML (Office Open XML)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — An ISO/IEC standard (29500) for office document formats. DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX are all OOXML formats. The specification defines the XML schemas used to represent document content, formatting, and metadata.
texts.text: — An ISO/IEC standard (29500) for office document formats. DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX are all OOXML formats. The specification defines the XML schemas used to represent document content, formatting, and metadata.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: python-docx
texts.text: python-docx

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — A Python library for creating and updating Microsoft Word DOCX files. It provides an API for adding paragraphs, tables, images, and styles to Word documents programmatically.
texts.text: — A Python library for creating and updating Microsoft Word DOCX files. It provides an API for adding paragraphs, tables, images, and styles to Word documents programmatically.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Content Control
texts.text: Content Control

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — An interactive element in a Word document that provides a structured region for user input. Content controls include text boxes, date pickers, dropdown lists, and checkboxes.
texts.text: — An interactive element in a Word document that provides a structured region for user input. Content controls include text boxes, date pickers, dropdown lists, and checkboxes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Document Part
texts.text: Document Part

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — A component of a DOCX file package. DOCX files are ZIP archives containing multiple XML parts including the main document, styles, fonts, headers, footers, and media files.
texts.text: — A component of a DOCX file package. DOCX files are ZIP archives containing multiple XML parts including the main document, styles, fonts, headers, footers, and media files.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Style Inheritance
texts.text: Style Inheritance

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — The mechanism by which paragraph and character styles in a DOCX document inherit properties from parent styles. Understanding style inheritance is essential for accurate formatting extraction.
texts.text: — The mechanism by which paragraph and character styles in a DOCX document inherit properties from parent styles. Understanding style inheritance is essential for accurate formatting extraction.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Run
texts.text: Run

texts.label: text
texts.orig: — A contiguous region of text within a paragraph that shares the same formatting properties. A single paragraph may contain multiple runs with different fonts, sizes, or colors.
texts.text: — A contiguous region of text within a paragraph that shares the same formatting properties. A single paragraph may contain multiple runs with different fonts, sizes, or colors.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 6. Mixed Content with Lists
texts.text: 6. Mixed Content with Lists

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Real-world documents often combine prose paragraphs with different list types. This section demonstrates that pattern:
texts.text: Real-world documents often combine prose paragraphs with different list types. This section demonstrates that pattern:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When processing DOCX files programmatically, there are several key challenges to be aware of:
texts.text: When processing DOCX files programmatically, there are several key challenges to be aware of:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: List detection: Distinguishing between bulleted lists, numbered lists, and indented paragraphs requires inspection of the paragraph's numbering properties.
texts.text: List detection: Distinguishing between bulleted lists, numbered lists, and indented paragraphs requires inspection of the paragraph's numbering properties.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Nesting depth: Multi-level lists use numbering definition references that map abstract numbering IDs to concrete level formats.
texts.text: Nesting depth: Multi-level lists use numbering definition references that map abstract numbering IDs to concrete level formats.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Style preservation: Converting lists to HTML or Markdown requires mapping Word's list styles to the target format's list syntax.
texts.text: Style preservation: Converting lists to HTML or Markdown requires mapping Word's list styles to the target format's list syntax.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following numbered steps outline a recommended approach for handling these challenges:
texts.text: The following numbered steps outline a recommended approach for handling these challenges:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Inspect each paragraph's numbering properties to determine if it belongs to a list.
texts.text: Inspect each paragraph's numbering properties to determine if it belongs to a list.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Group consecutive list paragraphs by their numbering ID to identify list boundaries.
texts.text: Group consecutive list paragraphs by their numbering ID to identify list boundaries.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Map the numbering level to the appropriate output format (e.g., <ul>/<ol> nesting in HTML).
texts.text: Map the numbering level to the appropriate output format (e.g., <ul>/<ol> nesting in HTML).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Preserve any inline formatting (bold, italic, hyperlinks) within list items.
texts.text: Preserve any inline formatting (bold, italic, hyperlinks) within list items.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By following this approach, you can reliably extract and convert lists from DOCX documents while maintaining their semantic structure and visual formatting.
texts.text: By following this approach, you can reliably extract and convert lists from DOCX documents while maintaining their semantic structure and visual formatting.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This sample DOCX file is provided by Sample-Files.com. Visit us for more sample files and resource.
texts.text: This sample DOCX file is provided by Sample-Files.com. Visit us for more sample files and resource.
